name: Gaiam Essentials Thick Yoga Mat with Carry Strap
whatWorks: 10mm of NBR foam provides genuine joint cushioning for knees, hips, and ankles during floor-based exercises — meaningful for yoga beginners who aren't yet used to kneeling and sitting on firm surfaces.
The dual non-slip texture on both sides prevents the mat from sliding on hardwood and tile floors during dynamic movements, which is the primary failure point of cheaper mats that slide underfoot.
The included carry strap makes it portable without buying a separate bag — practical for yoga classes, outdoor sessions, or moving between rooms.

At under $30 CAD, the Essentials mat is an accessible entry point for yoga, stretching, and floor-based workouts without financial commitment — the right starting price for someone testing whether a home yoga practice will stick.
worthKnowing: The grip degrades noticeably when the mat gets wet with sweat. Hot yoga or sweaty power yoga sessions require a yoga towel laid over the top — the mat alone will become slippery mid-practice.
NBR foam degrades faster than natural rubber or TPE alternatives — expect visible compression and surface wear within 12–18 months of regular daily use. Premium mats last significantly longer.
The mat has a pronounced plastic smell when new that takes 3–5 sessions to dissipate. Airing it outside for 24–48 hours before first use helps.
At 24" wide, the mat is narrower than some users prefer for lying-down stretches and poses — taller users may also find the 68" length tight for Savasana.

commonComplaints.title: Slippery when sweaty…
commonComplaints.body: It's fine for a gentle morning yoga session but the second I start sweating in a power yoga class it becomes a slip hazard. You need a towel for hot or vigorous yoga.

commonComplaints.title: Plastic smell takes time to fade…
commonComplaints.body: The smell was strong enough that my partner made me keep it outside for a week. It does go away eventually but it's unpleasant at first.

commonComplaints.title: Shows compression wear after a year…
commonComplaints.body: A year of daily use and I can see where my knees and feet land every session — the foam has compressed there and doesn't spring back. Not a premium mat.

commonComplaints.title: Narrower than expected…
commonComplaints.body: I'm 6'2" and my shoulders hang off the sides in Warrior II. At 24" wide it's fine for standing poses but not ideal for lying work.

verdict: The Gaiam Essentials is the right yoga mat for beginners who want to start a yoga or floor exercise practice without spending $80–150 on a premium mat before knowing if they'll stick with it. The 10mm cushioning is genuinely comfortable for joints, the carry strap is a practical inclusion, and the price removes any barrier to starting. The honest limitations — sweat-induced slipperiness and NBR foam durability — are real but appropriate for the price tier. Committed practitioners will upgrade to natural rubber or TPE within a year; casual users may never need to.

realBuyers.quote: I've been doing yoga for 3 months on this and my knees finally don't hurt. The extra cushioning made all the difference.
Rolls up small, strap keeps it together, fits in my gym bag. Perfect for class.
I use it for morning stretching every day. Simple, does the job, no complaints for the price.
I wasn't sure if I'd stick with yoga so I bought this as a starter. A year later I'm still using it and thinking about upgrading.

metaDescription: Gaiam Essentials yoga mat review for Canada: 10mm NBR foam, non-slip, carry strap included. Honest on sweat-slip and foam durability. ~$29 CAD.
introText: The Gaiam Essentials Thick Yoga Mat offers 10mm of joint-cushioning NBR foam with a non-slip surface on both sides and an included carry strap — the accessible starting point for home yoga, stretching, and floor workouts. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07N4RPSNS) at around $29 CAD.
